folio.label: 77r
folio.l: In vndermeles , and in morwenynges
And seitú hise matynes , & his holy thynges
As he gotú , in his lymytaciouö
Wommen moun go now , safly vp & dounö
In euäy bussú , and vnder euäy tree
There is noon other , Incubus but he
And he ne wyl do hem , but dishonour
¢ And so byfel , that this kyngö Arthour
Had in his hous , a lusty bacheler
That on a day , cam ridyngö fro ryuer
And happed that allone , as he was bornö
He saw a mayde , walkyngö hi~ byforn~
Of whiche mayde , a noon maugre hire hede
By verray force , byraft hire maydenhede
For whicú oppression , was swicú clamour
And swicú pursute , vn to the kyngö Arthour
That dampned was this knygút , for to be deed
By cours of lawe , and shuld han loost his heed
îauenture , swicú was the statute thoo
But that the Quene , and othere ladies moo
So longe preyeden , the kyngö of grace
Til he his lyf , hi~ gunted in the place
And ©af hi~ to the Quene , al at hire wylle
To chese wheither she wolde , hi~ saue or spille
The Quene thanketú the kyngö , w al hire mygút
And aftir this , thus spak she to the knygút
Whan that she saw hire tyme , vp on a day
Thow stondest ©et q¶ she , in swicú array
That of thy lif , ©et hast æè non seurte
I gunte the lif , if thow canst telle me

note.notetxt: 877Interruption in the text. A different pen and a lighter ink.ODR

folio.l: What thyngö is it , that wommen most desiren
Be war , & kepe thyn nekke bon , fromö Iren~
And if æè canst nat , telle it me anoon
ñet wol I ©eue the , leue for to goon
A twelwemonetú & a day , to seche & leere

note.notetxt: 882A mark on the left margin goes from line 872 to 882, made in brwon ink. Seems the same of the text.ODR

folio.l: An answere sufficeant , in this matere
And seurte wol I haue , er that thow pace
Thy body for to ©elden , in this place
Wo was this knygút , and sorwefully he syketú
But what , he may nat do , al as hi~ liketú
And at the last , he ches hi~ for to wende
And come a geyn , rigút at the ©eres ende
Witú swicú answere , as god hi~ wold purueye
And taketú his leue , and wendetú fortú his weye
He seketú euäy hous , and euäy place
